title: Beef Anticuchos with Grilled Vegetables
description: A simple and flavorful Peruvian-inspired dish, featuring marinated beef skewers grilled to perfection and served with a variety of roasted vegetables.

ingredients.name: Beef (1 pound)
ingredients.notes: Skirt steak or flank steak work well for anticuchos

ingredients.name: Delicate Onion
ingredients.notes: Thinly slice the onion for grilling

ingredients.name: Crisp Bell Pepper
ingredients.notes: Any color bell pepper works well for this recipe

ingredients.name: Peruvian Aji Amarillo Pepper
ingredients.notes: This pepper is a key flavor component in Peruvian cuisine

ingredients.name: Anticuchos Sauce
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought or homemade Aji Amarillo sauce

ingredients.name: Lime Juice
ingredients.notes: Fresh lime juice adds brightness to the dish

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for the marinade

instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine the Aji Amarillo pepper, lime juice, garlic, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, whisk together the marinade, beef, and anticuchos sauce. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Thread the marinated beef onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the beef for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare.
While the beef is cooking, brush the onion and bell pepper with oil and season with salt and black pepper. Grill the vegetables until they are tender and lightly charred.
Once the beef is cooked, let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Serve the beef with the grilled vegetables and a side of your favorite Peruvian-inspired dishes, such as Inca corn or fried yuca.
Garnish with fresh cilantro, if desired.
